page.content: The report presents calculated serum CRP threshold values and their correlation with endoscopic recurrence in a study of 63 patients. The CRP preoperative level is recorded at 4.0 mg/l (Figure 4a) with a p-value of P=0.15.

lines.text: The report presents calculated serum CRP threshold values and their correlation with endoscopic recurrence in a study of 63
patients. The CRP preoperative level is recorded at 4.0 mg/l (Figure 4a) with a p-value of P=0.15.

page.html:
Calculated serum CRP threshold valuesCorrelation with endoscopic recurrence (n=63)
CRP preop 4.0 mg/l(Figure 4a)P=0.15
CRP postop (POD1) 39.8 mg/l(Figure 4b)P=0.008

page.content: Postoperatively on day 1 (POD1), the CRP level is significantly increased to 39.8 mg/l (Figure 4b) with a p-value of P=0.008. By discharge on POD5, the CRP level decreases to 23.2 mg/l (Figure 4c) with a p-value of P=0.03, indicating a significant reduction from POD1. The CRP increase from preoperative to POD1 is noted as 27.5 mg/l (Figure 4d) with a significant p-value of P=0.03, while the CRP decrease from POD1 to POD5 is 21.9 mg/l (Figure 4e) with a p-value of P=0.07, showing a trend towards improvement but lacking statistical significance. In the second table, the polymorphism data is divided into two triglyceride groups: the low triglyceride group (≤ 2.2 mmol/l) includes 74 individuals (77.1%) with T/T, 18 individuals (18.8%) with T/C, and 4 individuals (4.2%) with C/C, totaling 96 (100%). The high triglyceride group (> 2.2 mmol/l) consists of 26 individuals (59.1%) with T/T, 18 individuals (40.9%) with T/C, and no individuals with C/C, totaling 44 (100%).
